pr_description:
Even with regex and comments supporting whitespaces between attributes and values (`<tag key = val />`), the code itself does not account for these spaces and includes them into final strings generating inconsistent parsing like `"foo` instead of `foo` or ` class` instead of `class`...

I added very lightweight change to `trim()` leading and trailing spaces if any.
See #161 

Plus I wrote test cases to prevent any future regression.
commit_message: add support for spaces between attributes and values

pr_description:
Even with regex and comments supporting whitespaces between attributes and values (`<tag key = val />`), the code itself does not account for these spaces and includes them into final strings generating inconsistent parsing like `"foo` instead of `foo` or ` class` instead of `class`...

I added very lightweight change to `trim()` leading and trailing spaces if any.
See #161 

Plus I wrote test cases to prevent any future regression.

refactor: improve inline code performance

> [!NOTE]
> Major v9 rewrite: new `parser` API and split entry points (`/react`, `/html`, `/markdown`), migrated build to Bun/bunup, revamped demo site, and added perf/tooling scripts.
> 
> - **Core/API**:
>   - Introduce `parser` API and split exports into `markdown-to-jsx/react`, `/html`, and `/markdown`.
>   - Update package exports/types; remove legacy CJS d.ts layout; raise Node engine to >=18 and React peer to >=16.
> - **Build/Release**:
>   - Migrate build to Bun/bunup; add custom plugin to strip debug/metrics code.
>   - Adjust `bunfig.toml`, `bunup.config.ts`, scripts, and CI/release workflows.
> - **Website/Docs**:
>   - Replace legacy demo with new Vite-based site (`site.tsx`, Tailwind, KaTeX, hljs); update `docs/index.html` and assets.
> - **Tooling/Perf**:
>   - Add benchmarking, profiling, metrics, and analysis scripts; generate entities and large fixtures.
> - **Housekeeping**:
>   - Update package metadata; remove large legacy tests/snapshots and obsolete files.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit ce6d1734ba914b7dd78ecfc00744175020d80a32. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commit_message:
rewrite for speed and spec compliance

markdown-to-jsx is now 100% gfm & commonmark compliant, while being
more than 10x faster depending on the benchmark at all input
sizes

it is the fastest markdown parser... maybe ever? definitely the
fastest javascript-based markdown parser

to achieve this there were some tradeoffs, notably the bundle size

if we dropped html entity compliance down to the absolute bare minimum,
the library would be much smaller and still much larger than earlier majors

however, I think in 2025 the performance is more worthwhile and enables fun
scenarios like butter-smooth live authoring

plus now you can use the parser directly and build your own renderer if you want

> [!NOTE]
> Major v9 rewrite: new `parser` API and split entry points (`/react`, `/html`, `/markdown`), migrated build to Bun/bunup, revamped demo site, and added perf/tooling scripts.
> 
> - **Core/API**:
>   - Introduce `parser` API and split exports into `markdown-to-jsx/react`, `/html`, and `/markdown`.
>   - Update package exports/types; remove legacy CJS d.ts layout; raise Node engine to >=18 and React peer to >=16.
> - **Build/Release**:
>   - Migrate build to Bun/bunup; add custom plugin to strip debug/metrics code.
>   - Adjust `bunfig.toml`, `bunup.config.ts`, scripts, and CI/release workflows.
> - **Website/Docs**:
>   - Replace legacy demo with new Vite-based site (`site.tsx`, Tailwind, KaTeX, hljs); update `docs/index.html` and assets.
> - **Tooling/Perf**:
>   - Add benchmarking, profiling, metrics, and analysis scripts; generate entities and large fixtures.
> - **Housekeeping**:
>   - Update package metadata; remove large legacy tests/snapshots and obsolete files.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit ce6d1734ba914b7dd78ecfc00744175020d80a32. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commit_message:
rewrite for speed and spec compliance

markdown-to-jsx is now 100% gfm & commonmark compliant, while being
more than 10x faster depending on the benchmark at all input
sizes

it is the fastest markdown parser... maybe ever? definitely the
fastest javascript-based markdown parser

to achieve this there were some tradeoffs, notably the bundle size

if we dropped html entity compliance down to the absolute bare minimum,
the library would be much smaller and still much larger than earlier majors

however, I think in 2025 the performance is more worthwhile and enables fun
scenarios like butter-smooth live authoring

plus now you can use the parser directly and build your own renderer if you want

> [!NOTE]
> Major v9 rewrite: new `parser` API and split entry points (`/react`, `/html`, `/markdown`), migrated build to Bun/bunup, revamped demo site, and added perf/tooling scripts.
> 
> - **Core/API**:
>   - Introduce `parser` API and split exports into `markdown-to-jsx/react`, `/html`, and `/markdown`.
>   - Update package exports/types; remove legacy CJS d.ts layout; raise Node engine to >=18 and React peer to >=16.
> - **Build/Release**:
>   - Migrate build to Bun/bunup; add custom plugin to strip debug/metrics code.
>   - Adjust `bunfig.toml`, `bunup.config.ts`, scripts, and CI/release workflows.
> - **Website/Docs**:
>   - Replace legacy demo with new Vite-based site (`site.tsx`, Tailwind, KaTeX, hljs); update `docs/index.html` and assets.
> - **Tooling/Perf**:
>   - Add benchmarking, profiling, metrics, and analysis scripts; generate entities and large fixtures.
> - **Housekeeping**:
>   - Update package metadata; remove large legacy tests/snapshots and obsolete files.
>

commit_message:
rewrite for speed and spec compliance

markdown-to-jsx is now 100% gfm & commonmark compliant, while being
more than 10x faster depending on the benchmark at all input
sizes

it is the fastest markdown parser... maybe ever? definitely the
fastest javascript-based markdown parser

to achieve this there were some tradeoffs, notably the bundle size

if we dropped html entity compliance down to the absolute bare minimum,
the library would be much smaller and still much larger than earlier majors

however, I think in 2025 the performance is more worthwhile and enables fun
scenarios like butter-smooth live authoring

plus now you can use the parser directly and build your own renderer if you want

> [!NOTE]
> Major v9 rewrite: new `parser` API and split entry points (`/react`, `/html`, `/markdown`), migrated build to Bun/bunup, revamped demo site, and added perf/tooling scripts.
> 
> - **Core/API**:
>   - Introduce `parser` API and split exports into `markdown-to-jsx/react`, `/html`, and `/markdown`.
>   - Update package exports/types; remove legacy CJS d.ts layout; raise Node engine to >=18 and React peer to >=16.
> - **Build/Release**:
>   - Migrate build to Bun/bunup; add custom plugin to strip debug/metrics code.
>   - Adjust `bunfig.toml`, `bunup.config.ts`, scripts, and CI/release workflows.
> - **Website/Docs**:
>   - Replace legacy demo with new Vite-based site (`site.tsx`, Tailwind, KaTeX, hljs); update `docs/index.html` and assets.
> - **Tooling/Perf**:
>   - Add benchmarking, profiling, metrics, and analysis scripts; generate entities and large fixtures.
> - **Housekeeping**:
>   - Update package metadata; remove large legacy tests/snapshots and obsolete files.
>

commit_message:
rewrite for speed and spec compliance

markdown-to-jsx is now 100% gfm & commonmark compliant, while being
more than 10x faster depending on the benchmark at all input
sizes

it is the fastest markdown parser... maybe ever? definitely the
fastest javascript-based markdown parser

to achieve this there were some tradeoffs, notably the bundle size

if we dropped html entity compliance down to the absolute bare minimum,
the library would be much smaller and still much larger than earlier majors

however, I think in 2025 the performance is more worthwhile and enables fun
scenarios like butter-smooth live authoring

plus now you can use the parser directly and build your own renderer if you want

pr_description: Replace rule-based markdown parser with a compact table-driven parser. Parsing is 27-82% faster depending on input size and bundle size is reduced by ~25% (gzip). Improved CommonMark compliance for HTML block handling and streaming mode reliability. No public API changes.
commit_message:
feat: compact table-driven parser with optimized hot paths

Replace rule-based markdown parser with a compact table-driven parser. Parsing is 27-82% faster depending on input size and bundle size is reduced by ~25% (gzip). Improved CommonMark compliance for HTML block handling and streaming mode reliability. No API changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit_message:
feat: compact table-driven parser with optimized hot paths

Replace rule-based markdown parser with a compact table-driven parser. Parsing is 27-82% faster depending on input size and bundle size is reduced by ~25% (gzip). Improved CommonMark compliance for HTML block handling and streaming mode reliability. No API changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: bypass React.createElement for ~2x faster JSX output

Construct raw React element objects ({$$typeof, type, key, ref, props})
directly instead of calling React.createElement, avoiding type validation,
key/ref extraction, defaultProps merging, and dev-mode freezing overhead.

Auto-detects the $$typeof symbol from the installed React version for
forward compatibility (React 18, 19, and future versions). Falls back to
createElement when opts.createElement is provided.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+    const detector = /(:[^:]+:)/g
+
+    const replaceEmoji = (text: string): React.ReactNode => {
+      return text.split(detector).map((part, index) => {
+        if (part.startsWith(':') && part.endsWith(':')) {
+          const shortcode = part.slice(1, -1)
+
+          return {shortcodeMap[shortcode] || part}

+        return part
+      })

+    render(

+        renderRule(next, node) {

+
+          return next()
